body.title: agent_datacenter: v1 access control — halt and block require rack.* or self
body.result: AuthError added to exceptions.py. Skeleton._check_caller_auth() enforces from_device==skeleton|self for halt/block. {did}_halt and {did}_block MCP tools registered per device. v1 localhost trust noted in docstring.
body.description:
Enforce that halt() and block() can only be called by rack.* (the skeleton) or the device itself. Stub — full auth is Phase 5+. Enforce from day one because adding auth later to an unauthed system is painful.

Proposed shape:
- In Skeleton: intercept incoming halt() and block() calls
  Check envelope.from_device: must be 'skeleton' or the target device_id
  Otherwise: raise AuthError('Unauthorized: halt/block requires rack.* or self')
  AuthError is logged with from_device, target, and timestamp
- Stub: no cryptographic verification — caller identity is from envelope.from_device (trust on localhost for v1)
- Note stub limitation in docs: 'v1 trusts caller identity from envelope; cryptographic ACL is Phase 5+'

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/skeleton/skeleton.py (intercept halt/block)
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/skeleton/exceptions.py (AuthError)

Design rules:
- docs-in-code: comment noting v1 trust model and Phase 5 upgrade path

Scope boundary:
IN: from_device check on halt/block, AuthError exception
OUT: cryptographic verification (Phase 5+), other operation auth (restart, recovery allowed for all callers in v1)

Test plan:
- Envelope(from_device='skeleton', ...) calling halt() → allowed
- Envelope(from_device='igor-wild-0001', ...) calling halt() on igor → allowed (self)
- Envelope(from_device='CC.0', ...) calling halt() on igor → AuthError raised
- AuthError is logged
